text: What should you do immediately if you witness a crew member fall completely overboard into the sea?

options.text: Run to the bridge to inform the captain first
Release the nearest lifebuoy immediately on the side the person has fallen
Look for a throwing line in the deck locker
Shout down to the engine room to stop the engines

explanation: When a person falls overboard, the immediate priority is to mark their position and aid their buoyancy. Releasing the lifebuoy (which usually features a smoke signal and light) provides a visual reference for the bridge and a life-saving aid for the survivor.
